title: Testing, Release Gates, and Memory Incident Defense · Failure Case: Memory Incident Response
summary: A critical native agent must ship a memory rewrite with compatibility, rollback, observability, and practiced incident recovery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem memory incident response increment in production memory-safety defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preserve first-failure artifacts, symbolize safely, contain exposure, reproduce, repair causally, and verify recovery and prevention.
Canary immutable artifacts against memory, crash, latency, allocator, and correctness signals with a tested rollback trigger.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Memory Incident Response · Theory Memory Incident Response
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8516. Produce bounded C evidence to decide release readiness from complementary memory evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Build Memory Incident Response evidence · Theory Memory Incident Response
steps.instruction:
